---
title: "Create US Mobility Report csv"
author: "Tony Carilli"
date: "8/11/2020"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)

```

# Read Global Mobility Report

Global Mobility Report exceeds github file size

```{r global-mobility-report}
global_mobility_report <- 
here::here("data", "Global_Mobility_Report.csv") %>% 
  read_csv(col_types =
      cols(
        country_region_code = col_character(),
        country_region = col_character(),
        sub_region_1 = col_character(),
        sub_region_2 = col_character(),
        metro_area = col_character(),
        iso_3166_2_code = col_character(),
        census_fips_code = col_integer(),
        date = col_date(format = ""),
        retail_and_recreation_percent_change_from_baseline = col_double(),
        grocery_and_pharmacy_percent_change_from_baseline = col_double(),
        parks_percent_change_from_baseline = col_double(),
        transit_stations_percent_change_from_baseline = col_double(),
        workplaces_percent_change_from_baseline = col_double(),
        residential_percent_change_from_baseline = col_double()
      )
  )
```

# Create US Mobility Report csv 

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r us-mobility-report}
us_mobility <- 
global_mobility_report %>% 
  filter(country_region_code == "US") %>% 
  select(-country_region, -metro_area) %>% 
  rename(state = sub_region_1, county = sub_region_2, fips = census_fips_code) %>% 
write_csv(us_mobility, "data/us_mobility_report.csv")
```


```{r}
global_mobility_report %>% 
  filter(country_region_code == "US") %>% 
  select(-country_region, -metro_area) %>% 
  rename(state = sub_region_1, county = sub_region_2, fips = census_fips_code) %>% 
  pivot_longer(contains("percent"),
               values_to = "percent_change_from_baseline",
               names_to = "mobility_index") %>% 
  filter(county == "Prince Edward County") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = percent_change_from_baseline, color = mobility_index)) + 
  geom_line()
```
